title: Code Review Findings
description: Output schema for the codereview skill. Contains envelope metadata and an array of findings from deterministic and AI review passes.

review_mode.description: Whether the review used standard mode or chunked mode for large changesets

provenance.description: How the reviewed code was produced. Affects review rigor level.

chunk_count.description: Number of review chunks (null or omitted in standard mode)

chunks.description: Chunk metadata for chunked reviews (null or omitted in standard mode)

description.description: Directory path(s) covered by this chunk

file_count.description: Number of files in this chunk

diff_lines.description: Total lines changed in this chunk

risk_tier.description: Highest risk tier of any file in this chunk

passes_run.description: Number of explorer passes that reviewed this chunk

findings.description: Number of findings from explorers for this chunk

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this review run (ISO timestamp + short hash)

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the review started

scope.description: What kind of diff was reviewed

base_ref.description: Base reference (branch name, commit SHA, or PR base)

head_ref.description: Head reference (branch name, commit SHA, or PR head)

pr_number.description: PR number if reviewing a pull request, null otherwise

files_reviewed.description: List of file paths included in the diff

verdict.description: Overall merge readiness verdict. PASS: no must-fix issues. WARN: has should-fix issues but no blockers. FAIL: has must-fix issues that block merge.

strengths.description: 2-3 things done well in this change, identified by the review judge

spec_gaps.description: Requirements from the spec not addressed by the diff. Empty array if no spec was provided. Derived from spec_requirements for backward compatibility.

id.description: Stable requirement ID: REQ-001, REQ-002, etc.

text.description: The requirement text as extracted from the spec

source_section.description: The spec section/heading this requirement was found in

priority.description: Priority inferred from language: must/shall=must, should=should, could/may=could, else informational

impl_status.description: Whether the diff addresses this requirement

impl_evidence.description: File:line references showing where the requirement is implemented, or why it is not

impl_files.description: Files in the diff that implement this requirement

status.description: Overall test coverage status for this requirement

category_evidence.description: Why this test was classified in this category

needed_categories.description: Test categories that should exist for this requirement but are missing

category_gap_reason.description: Why specific test categories are needed (e.g., 'Crosses service boundary, needs integration test')

spec_requirements.description: Per-requirement implementation and test coverage traceability. Populated when --spec is provided, empty array otherwise.

tool_status.description: Status of each tool/pass that was attempted. Keys include deterministic scan tools (semgrep, trivy, rubocop, brakeman, pmd, etc.), AI passes (ai_correctness, ai_security, etc.), and coverage tools (coverage_go, coverage_python, coverage_rust, coverage_typescript, coverage_ruby, coverage_java).

source.description: Whether this came from a deterministic tool or an AI pass

pass.description: Which review pass produced this finding

confidence.description: Confidence score. AI findings below 0.65 are suppressed.

sources.description: Optional provenance list for deduplicated findings (e.g., ["semgrep", "sonarqube"]).

evidence.description: Concrete evidence: code snippet, tool output, or reproduction steps

evidence_source.description: How the evidence was obtained. Must reference a specific tool call (e.g., 'Read src/auth/login.py:45-60') or cross-file context. If assumption-based, must state the assumption.

failure_mode.description: What breaks and when. Required for high/critical severity.

tests_to_add.description: Suggested test scenarios to cover this finding

test_category_needed.description: Which categories of tests are needed for this finding

test_level.description: Test pyramid level classification (L0=Contract through L5=E2E). Populated by test-adequacy findings.

bug_finding_level.description: Bug-finding test level (BF1=Property through BF8=Backward-compat). Populated by test-adequacy findings.

gap_reason.description: Why the identified test level is needed for this specific code. Populated by test-adequacy findings.

action_tier.description: Action tier: must_fix (critical/high, conf>=0.80), should_fix (medium or high 0.65-0.79), consider (everything else above confidence floor)

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint (12 hex chars) for cross-review tracking

lifecycle_status.description: Whether this finding appeared in a previous review

pre_existing.description: True when the bug exists in unchanged code but is surfaced because the diff creates a new path to it. Default false (omitted = introduced by diff).

pre_existing_newly_reachable.description: True when a pre-existing bug existed before but the diff creates a new code path to trigger it. Only meaningful when pre_existing is true.

suppressed_findings.description: Findings suppressed via .codereview-suppressions.json (rejected or deferred). Same shape as findings but with lifecycle_status of rejected or deferred.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint (12 hex chars) for cross-review tracking

lifecycle_status.description: Suppression status: rejected (permanently dismissed) or deferred (temporarily dismissed)

lifecycle_summary.description: Counts of findings by lifecycle status

new.description: Findings appearing for the first time

recurring.description: Findings that appeared in a previous review

tier_summary.description: Count of findings per action tier

_timing.description: Observability timing data for the review pipeline. Optional — omit if timing.sh was not available.
